commit.message:
fix: apply security hardening across extension

- restrict content script matches to http/https only (manifest.json)
- validate message sender id and rules type in onMessage (content.js)
- replace cssText injection with individual style property setters (content.js)
- add MAX_MATCHES_PER_NODE limit to prevent ReDoS freeze (content.js)
- add console.warn for unknown rule types and regex compile failures (content.js)
- add validateRule() schema check on storage load (popup.js)
- guard against non-array storage data in loadRules (popup.js)
- set rule color dot via .style.backgroundColor instead of innerHTML (popup.js)
- add spectra change: security-hardening (archived)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: reduce cognitive complexity in TimelineUrlBuilder (java:S3776)

Extract private buildQueryString() helper so buildFilterRemoveLinks()
delegates the repeated StringBuilder loop instead of inlining four
copies. Complexity drops from 73 to well below the 15-unit limit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add agent-inbox .NET 10 CLI tool

- SQLite-backed inter-agent messaging with WAL mode and foreign keys
- Commands: register, deregister, agents, send, reply, inbox, read
- Three output formats: plain (tabular), json, ndjson
- Soft-delete support for agents (deregistered_at)
- Reply threading via reply_to_id
- AOT-compatible JSON serialization via source-generated JsonContext
- 6 xUnit integration tests covering all core database operations
- Global --db-path and --format options via AddGlobalOption()

commit.message:
Complete Metal.jl GPU acceleration

- Fix run_gpu! loop to follow exact 16-step CPU integration order
- Fix recovery duration: use lognormal (matching CPU default) instead of exponential
- Fix dynamic network support: re-generate edges each step (was using static cached edges)
- Add SEIR GPU support (exposed→infected transition, recovery time setting)
- Fix SIS GPU: use ti_recovered-based recovery, add immunity waning + boost
- Add cache_edges option for static network optimization
- Optimize results: GPU-side sum() instead of full state download
- GPUDiseaseArrays extended with exposed/ti_exposed fields
- Add _sample_recovery_durations helper matching CPU distribution
- Correctness verified: r>0.999 for SIR, SIS, SEIR (30 seeds)
- Update GPU vignette with actual benchmarks and working examples
- Extension now 1,696 lines (was 1,385)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Julia port of the Starsim agent-based disease modeling framework

commit.message:
docs(k8s): add README with infra/observability prerequisites; add OTEL endpoint to configmap

Documents that PostgreSQL, Keycloak, immudb, and the full observability
stack (Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, Tempo, OTel Collector) are external
infrastructure — not deployed as k8s pods. Provides Helm quick-start,
secrets creation guide, ENCRYPTION_KEY warning, and port reference.

Adds OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT placeholder to base configmap so
overlays can override it per environment.

commit.message:
fix(person-registry): use JdbcTemplate in seeders to bypass Hibernate state detection

Replace JPA repository.save() with JdbcTemplate INSERT ... ON CONFLICT to avoid
Hibernate 6.x PropertyValueException when persisting entities with explicitly-set
UUIDs and @Version/@GeneratedValue annotations.

Also adds JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS to payment-service to disable grpc-netty native epoll
transport, resolving the SIGSEGV (exit code 139) on container startup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(person-registry): add demo seed data for persons and demo creditor orgs

DemoPersonSeeder (seed-persons=true):
  Inserts 4 fictional Danish debtors with explicit UUIDs matching
  debt-service and case-service seed migrations:
    d...001  Lars Andersen    (SKAT, crossing debts)
    d...002  Mads Petersen    (TOLD + underholdsbidrag)
    d...003  Emma Nielsen     (SU-gaeld + strafboede)
    d...004  Jens Moeller     (ejendomsskat, disputed)
  All PII AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest. Idempotent (skips if hash exists).

DemoOrganizationSeeder:
  Added two demo creditor orgs at the top of the seed list with UUIDs
  matching debt-service creditor_org_id references:
    00000000-...-001  Skattestyrelsen (Demo)   CVR 99000001
    00000000-...-002  Toldstyrelsen (Demo)     CVR 99000002
  Follows existing idempotent pattern (cvr_hash check).
  Added @Order(1) so org seeder always runs before person seeder.

docker-compose.yml:
  DEMO_SEED_ORGANIZATIONS=true, DEMO_SEED_PERSONS=true on person-registry.

application.yml:
  seed-persons: DOLLAR{DEMO_SEED_PERSONS:false} added alongside seed-organizations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add .github/workflows/docs.yml: builds and deploys docs on push to

- Add docs/build/ to .gitignore
- Add Documenter compat bound to docs/Project.toml

commit.message:
fix(ci): lower caseworker-portal JaCoCo thresholds after timeline extraction

The petition050 timeline refactoring moved buildFilter, matchesFilter,
buildLoadMoreUrl, and buildFilterRemoveLinks from CaseworkerTimelineController
into opendebt-common. Those branches now count toward opendebt-common
coverage (which has dedicated tests), not this module.

Lines:    0.44 → 0.40  (actual 0.41)
Branches: 0.43 → 0.34  (actual 0.35)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(common): add unit tests for TimelineFilterHelper and TimelineUrlBuilder

Covers all branches in matchesFilter, buildFilter, buildLoadMoreUrl, and
buildFilterRemoveLinks — restoring opendebt-common JaCoCo branch coverage
above the 0.16 threshold enforced in the module pom.xml.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: extract timeline filter/URL utilities to opendebt-common (duplication)

Create TimelineFilterHelper (buildFilter, matchesFilter) and
TimelineUrlBuilder (buildLoadMoreUrl, buildFilterRemoveLinks) in the
common timeline package. The 4 PARAM_* constants move into
TimelineUrlBuilder.

All 3 portal timeline controllers now delegate to these shared utilities
instead of carrying ~130 lines of identical private methods each.
No logic or behaviour changed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore(sonar): exclude generated SOAP stubs and SQL migrations from analysis

sonar.exclusions: JAXB-generated SOAP classes under soap/skat/generated/
  and soap/oio/generated/ are machine-generated and must not be touched.
  Removing them from all analysis eliminates ~541 spurious duplication lines.

sonar.cpd.exclusions: Flyway DDL and seed SQL files share CREATE TABLE
  and INSERT patterns by design. Structural similarity in migrations is
  unavoidable and not indicative of copy-paste debt.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: document intentionally empty methods (S1186)

- TestClsAuditCapture.flush(): no-op in test double (synchronous capture)
- TestClsAuditConfig (anonymous flush()): same reason
- Oces3SoapSecurityInterceptor.afterCompletion(): no cleanup needed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: extract duplicate string literals into constants (S1192)

EventCategoryMapper: 13 event-type constants (EVT_CASE_CREATED, etc.)
DebtServiceSoapClient: 5 header/error-code constants
ClsSoapAuditInterceptor: 4 message-context property key constants
CaseworkerTimelineController: 4 URL query-param constants
CitizenTimelineController: 4 URL query-param constants
CreditorTimelineController: 4 URL query-param constants

Resolves all 34 open S1192 CRITICAL issues. No logic changed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(security): prevent path traversal in WsdlServingFilter (S2083)

Validate service name against an explicit allowlist of known WSDL
services (skat, oio) before constructing the classpath resource path.
Any unrecognised service name falls through to the normal filter chain,
returning 404.

Fixes SonarQube BLOCKER javasecurity:S2083.

commit.message:
Fix critical security and logic bugs found in code review

Security Fixes:
- CRITICAL: Remove eval() for BUILD_CMD and TEST_CMD in patch_test.sh
  Replaced with bash -c to prevent command injection via env variables
- HIGH: Replace /tmp/build.log and /tmp/test.log with mktemp()
  Prevents race conditions and data corruption in multi-user systems

Logic & Validation Fixes:
- HIGH: Fix division by zero vulnerability in patch_check.sh calc_similarity()
  Added proper handling for zero adds/dels cases
- MEDIUM: Add input validation for silicon test results in patch_test.sh
  Only accepts PASS/FAIL/PENDING, defaults to PENDING on invalid input
- MEDIUM: Fix per-file diff stat calculation in patch_check.sh
  Use awk to track adds/dels per file instead of counting entire patch
- MEDIUM: Add validation for empty review branch in patch_check.sh
  Dies with clear error if branch name cannot be extracted from JSON
- LINUX-ONLY: Simplified stat command to use only Linux syntax (macOS support deferred)

All Python tests still pass (29/29 ✅)
All fixes tested for correctness

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: external-patch-handling

repository.name: external-patch-handling
repository.description: Python CLI pipeline for structured cross-company BIOS patch exchange (BHS). Covers receive, apply, functional equivalence check, test, review report, and integration.

commit.message:
Add bash versions of patch pipeline scripts

- patch_receive.sh: Validate and stage patches from a directory
- patch_apply.sh: Apply staged patches to a review branch
- patch_check.sh: Compare sender intent vs actual changes landed
- patch_test.sh: Run build and unit tests, collect silicon results
- patch_integrate.sh: Cherry-pick reviewed patches to working branch

All bash scripts:
- Provide zero-dependency alternatives to Python versions
- Generate compatible JSON output for downstream steps
- Support same configuration via .patch-pipeline.toml and env vars
- Can be mixed with Python scripts in the same pipeline
- Include comprehensive error handling and user-friendly output

Added BASH_README.md with detailed usage guide and examples.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Docs: add feature 005 admin UI pages plan

Adds implementation plan for feature 005 (admin UI pages).

Includes:
- Research decisions (10 items)
- Data model mapping
- Admin HTML route contracts (7 routes)
- Quickstart guide
- Updated copilot instructions

Created via speckit.plan pipeline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

Docs: align voucher terminology with backend model

- FR-014: use VoucherStatus values (unused, active, expired, revoked)
- FR-018: derive redemption status from redeemed_count field
- Update Voucher entity definition to match backend model

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

fix: ONNX export shape ignored; validate divisibility by patch_size (#876)

- Resolve patch_size from model_config by default (with fallback) and ensure
  explicit values match configuration across both export() and predict()
- Validate patch_size strictly:
  - must be a positive integer
  - reject bool values (True/False)
- Use block_size = patch_size * num_windows for all shape divisibility checks,
  matching backbone expectations

- Add robust shape validation:
  - enforce (H, W) arity and reject invalid types (bool, float)
  - require positive dimensions (prevent modulo passing negative/zero values)
  - ensure divisibility by block_size
  - validate default resolution when shape is not provided

- Improve error handling with clear, consistent ValueError messages
  across all validation paths

- Refactor validation logic:
  - extract _validate_shape_dims() for shared shape checks
  - extract _resolve_patch_size() for consistent patch_size handling
  - simplify export() and predict() by delegating to shared helpers

- Expand and clean up test coverage:
  - cover patch_size resolution, mismatch, and invalid values
  - cover shape validation (arity, type, positivity, divisibility)
  - add regression tests for rectangular input shapes
  - validate num_windows interactions and default resolution behavior
  - simplify and reorganize test structure for clarity

- Update docstrings to document patch_size behavior and validation rules

---------

commit.message:
Docs: address round 2 Copilot review on spec 005

- Document CSRF exemption for logout in FR-019 and FR-023
- Standardize voucher status to unredeemed/redeemed
- Split grants/vouchers API assumption to note voucher gap
- Clarify logout endpoint assumption wording

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

fix(ci): install local modules before OWASP scan

The security-scan job runs in a fresh environment and cannot resolve
opendebt-common from Maven Central (it's a local snapshot). Adding
mvn install -DskipTests installs all local modules to ~/.m2 so
dependency-check can resolve the full dependency graph.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Docs: address Copilot review feedback on spec 005

- Add voucher code column to Grants acceptance scenario 1
- Add cache-control acceptance scenario for Logout page
- Resolve logout endpoint ambiguity in Assumptions
- Align checklist wording with repo convention for technical context

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

Docs: add feature 005 admin UI pages specification

Add specification and requirements checklist for the admin UI pages
feature covering Dashboard, Grants, Vouchers, and Logout pages with
27 functional requirements and 7 success criteria.

Created via speckit.specify pipeline.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

fix(ci): make OWASP non-fatal on NVD update errors via -DfailOnError=false

If the NVD_API_KEY secret is not yet configured (or empty), the NVD data
feed update returns 403/404. Adding -DfailOnError=false allows the scan
to proceed with locally cached data rather than halting the build.
Once the NVD_API_KEY secret is set under Settings > Secrets > Actions,
the plugin will perform a full NVD update automatically.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: reject guest_external_url with non-root path

Base URL should not contain a path since /guest/authorize is
appended to it. Add validation and test for path rejection.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

fix(ci): restore SonarCloud and wire NVD API key for OWASP

- Remove continue-on-error from SonarCloud step: Automatic Analysis is
  now disabled in SonarCloud settings, so manual CI analysis works.
- Remove continue-on-error from OWASP step and pass NVD_API_KEY secret
  via -DnvdApiKey so the NVD data feed update succeeds without 403/404.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: track video status and results

I added a PostgreSQL database to the solution and implemented
persistence of video tracking data. When the `CreateVideo` function is
invoked, a new `Video` record is created in the database. After the
video is uploaded, the `ProcessVideo` function updates the corresponding
`Video` record with the Cloudflare Stream video ID and the current
status. The `CloudflareWebhook` function updates the `Video` record's
status based on the webhook events received from Cloudflare Stream. This
will allow us to track the status of video processing and handle any
issues that may arise. We can also start to implement the client
application features for viewing and playing back videos.

Closes: #19
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: mfcollins3 <104274+mfcollins3@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: UDP network chat (MFC/C++ school project)

Peer-to-peer UDP chat application built with Visual Studio 2008, MFC,
and Winsock. Features custom protocol with headers, checksum verification,
message fragmentation, and contact list management.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: tool-specific timeouts, circuit breaker in draft-assistant, logging

- Add per-tool timeout config: analytics/workflow tools get 25s, search 20s, default 15s
- Integrate circuit breaker into draft-assistant SSE endpoint
- Log conversation summarization failures instead of silently swallowing
- Record success/failure in circuit breaker for draft-assistant pipeline
